memoryArtifacts.headline: BIO
memoryArtifacts.description:
BIO:  Count of Ponthiu and Montbelliard, 1191-1221.  Commanded the right wing for the French at Bouvines, on the Albigensian Crusade.

** from http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/NORTHERN%20FRANCE.htm#Gui …

zh.lifeSummary: 當William IV Count of Ponthieu於從1166年到1178年出生在法國o-阿爾培斯艾朋提額時，他的父親Jean Ier de Ponthieu37歲，母親Beatrice St Pol24歲。他於1195年8月20日與Alais De France De Ponthieu在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1221年10月6日在他的家鄉，埋葬在法國皮卡迪索姆阿古勒斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William IV Count of Ponthieu于从1166年到1178年出生在法国o-阿爾培斯艾朋提額时，他的父亲Jean Ier de Ponthieu37岁，母亲Beatrice St Pol24岁。他于1195年8月20日与Alais De France De Ponthieu在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1221年10月6日在他的家乡，埋葬在法国皮卡迪索姆阿古勒斯。

nameMeanings.name: Count
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earl
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Ponthieu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: variant of Ponthieu, a habitational name from Ponthieu, a region (former county) in Picardy, between the rivers Authie and Somme.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When William IV Count of Ponthieu was born from 1166 to 1178, in Ponthieu, Ain, Rhône-Alpes, France, his father, Jean Ier de Ponthieu, was 37 and his mother, Beatrice St Pol, was 24. He married Alais De France De Ponthieu on 20 August 1195, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 6 October 1221, in his hometown, and was buried in Argoules, Somme, Picardie, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William IV Count of Ponthieu est né du 1166 au 1178, à Ponthieu, Ain, Rhône-Alpes, France, son père, Jean Ier de Ponthieu, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Beatrice St Pol, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Alais De France De Ponthieu le 20 août 1195, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 6 octobre 1221, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterré à Argoules, Somme, Picardie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als William IV Count of Ponthieu von 1166 bis 1178 in Ponthieu, Ain, Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean Ier de Ponthieu 37 und seine Mutter Beatrice St Pol 24. Er hat am 20. August 1195 in England Alais De France De Ponthieu geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 6. Oktober 1221 in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Argoules, Somme, Picardie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William IV Count of Ponthieu è nato il da 1166 a 1178 nel Ponthieu, Ain, Rhône-Alpes, Francia, il suo padre, Jean Ier de Ponthieu, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Beatrice St Pol, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Alais De France De Ponthieu il 20 agosto 1195, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 6 ottobre 1221, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolto a Argoules, Somme, Picardie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: William IV Count of Ponthieuがから1166年まで1178年にフランスルhoネ-アルペスエインポンスイエウで生まれた時，父Jean Ier de Ponthieuは37歳，母Beatrice St Polは24歳であった。1195年8月20日にイギリスでAlais De France De Ponthieuと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1221年10月6日に故郷で死去し，フランスピカーディソンメアルゴウレスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William IV Count of Ponthieu(이)가 1166년 부터 1178년 까지 프랑스 ho니-알프스 언 폰스이외에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean Ier de Ponthieu(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrice St Pol(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1195년 8월 20일 영국에서 Alais De France De Ponthieu(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1221년 10월 6일 그의 고향에서 사망했으며 프랑스 피카디 솜 아굴스에 묻혔다.

lv.from 1166 to 1178–6 October 1221: no 1166. gada līdz 1178. gadam–1221. gada 6. oktobris

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William IV Count of Ponthieu nasceu de 1166 a 1178, em Ponthieu, Ain, Rhône-Alpes, França, seu pai, Jean Ier de Ponthieu, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice St Pol, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Alais De France De Ponthieu em 20 de agosto de 1195, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 6 de outubro de 1221, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultado em Argoules, Somme, Picardie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William IV Count of Ponthieu родился с 1166 по 1178 в местности понтиеу, эин, рoне-алпесь, Франция, его отцу по имени Jean Ier de Ponthieu было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrice St Pol, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Alais De France De Ponthieu 20 августа 1195 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 6 октября 1221 г. в его родном городе и был похоронен в местности аргоулесь, сомм, пикарди, Франция.

sm.from 1166 to 1178–6 October 1221: mai le 1166 e o'o I le 1178–6 Oketopa 1221
sm.from 1166 to 1178: mai le 1166 e o'o I le 1178

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William IV Count of Ponthieu nació desde 1166 hasta 1178, en Ponthieu, Ain, Rhône-Alpes, Francia, su papá, Jean Ier de Ponthieu, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Beatrice St Pol, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Alais De France De Ponthieu el 20 de agosto de 1195, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 6 de octubre de 1221, en su pueblo, y fue sepultado en Argoules, Somme, Picardie, Francia.
